titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the R. Eric Gustafson Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday December 5, 2024 at 23:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English, with some materials in French and Spanish.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, professional and personal papers, photographs, printed material, computer and audiovisual media, and other papers documenting the career and life of American author, curator, and gallerist Eric Gustafson. Also included are correspondence, ephemera, and photographs of Navajo artist R. C. Gorman.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bolerium Books, 2010. Gift of Eric Gustafson, 2010, 2011. Purchased from Eric Gustafson 2016, 2017, and 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. July 2010 Acquisition, 1968-2009. II. November 2011 Acquisition, undated. III. November 2016 Acquisition, 1950-2016. IV. September 2017 Acquisition, 1934-2017. V. January 2019 Acquisition, circa 2011-2013.

bioghist.head: R. Eric Gustafson (1935-)
bioghist.p: R. Eric Gustafson is an American writer, curator, and gallerist. He is the author of two memoirs, Cinderella is a Man: A Picturesque Passage to Serenity (1998) and Last Guy Waltzing: A Tale of Reinvention (2013). Gustafson was born in 1935 in the Bronx, New York City. He received a B.A. from Queens College in 1957 and an M.F.A. in theater from Carnegie Mellon University in 1960. During the 1960s, he worked at the New York auction house Parke-Bernet Galleries as a consultant to high-profile clients such as Greta Garbo, Elizabeth Taylor, and Jacqueline Kennedy. Gustafson later managed art galleries, including the American Gallery of Theater Design in New York and the Jamison Gallery in Santa Fe, New Mexico. He curated 16 theater design exhibitions in New York, Santa Fe, and Italy, including "International Theater Designs" at Cooper-Hewitt Museum and "Designs for a Prima Donna: Dame Joan Sutherland" at Library for Performing Arts at the Lincoln Center. In 1984, he founded the Apollo Muses Center for the Arts in New Jersey, a non-profit presenting organization to showcase the work of young artists; the group held events and performances at Lu Shan Farm, an estate owned by his longtime friend, philanthropist Lila Hotz Luce Tyng. As a travel writer, he published numerous articles and two books about his journeys to India, India: Paradox and Treasures (2005) and Expect the Unexpected: Adventures of a Westerner Sitting in the Lap of Mother India (2009). Gustafson also authored the historical art book The Court Theaters of Europe (1982), which accompanied his lecture series of the same name. After many years of visiting Santa Fe for professional or personal reasons, he moved to the city permanently in 2006. In 2017, Gustafson published My Beloved Southwest : Scrapbook of articles, essays, photos and art covering a period of almost fifty years in The Land of Enchantment .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: R. Eric Gustafson Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, professional and personal papers, photographs, printed material, computer and audiovisual media, and other papers documenting the career and life of American author, curator, and gallerist Eric Gustafson. Also included are correspondence, ephemera, and photographs of Navajo artist R. C. Gorman. Among the other correspondents are artists Erté and Toshiko Takaezu, photographer Cecil Beaton, actors Celeste Holm and Ruth Warrick, opera singer Frederica von Stade, and philanthropist Lila Tyng. The collection contains drafts of works by Gustafson including Cinderella is a Man , Expect the Unexpected , India: Paradox and Treasures , Last Guy Waltzing , and My Beloved Southwest , in addition to "The Marchesa la marchetta chronicles," an essay about his friendship with Gorman. Other materials concern the Apollo Muses Center for the Arts, Gustafson's theater design exhibitions and work with galleries in New York and New Mexico, and his lecture series "The Court Theatres of Europe." The collection further contains scrapbooks and photograph albums related to his early life, career, and travels, as well as passports, journals, address and phone books, and calendars.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 10 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 11-15 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The R. Eric Gustafson Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

subject.part: Navajo Nation, Arizona, New Mexico & Utah

corpname.part: Apollo Muses Center for the Arts

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bolerium Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2010.

bioghist.head: R. C. Gorman (1931-2005)
bioghist.p: R. C. (Rudolph Carl) Gorman was a Native American artist of the Navajo Nation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence from R. C. Gorman to his friend Eric Gustafson, printed material, and photographs. Printed ephemera, some inscribed and signed by Gorman, includes cards with representations of Gorman's work, publicity, such as invitations to events, exhibition catalogs, and posters, and secondary material documenting Gorman's life, work, and popular reception.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of an account of Eric Gustafson's relationship with Navajo artist R. C. Gorman.

did.unittitle: "The Marchesa la marchetta chronicles," computer printout

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence, writings, professional and personal papers, photographs, electronic files, audiovisual material, and other papers of Eric Gustafson. Included are drafts, notes, ephemera, publicity, and correspondence for works by Gustafson such as Cinderella is a Man, Expect the Unexpected, Last Guy Waltzing, and India: Paradox and Treasures, in addition to a number of travel articles; papers related to the Apollo Muses Center for the Art; files on exhibitions curated by Gustafson; and slides and notes for his lecture series "The Court Theatres of Europe." The series contains correspondence and photographs of R. C. Gorman, Lila Tyng, and Ruth Warrick, as well as scrapbooks, material related to Gustafson's early life, and a diary from his stay at the Betty Ford Clinic.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Correspondence and Subject Files, Writings, Professional Papers, Personal Papers, Photographs and Slides, and Computer and Audiovisual Media.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Eric Gustafson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2016.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of files compiled by Eric Gustafson on friends and colleagues, such as R. C. Gorman, Celeste Holm, Lila Tyng, Tashiko Takaezu, Ruth Warrick, and Hugh Keelan and Frank Daykin, who served as music directors of the Apollo Muses Center. Each file contains an assortment of correspondence, photographs, and ephemera related to these individuals. Also included are several files of mixed correspondence with letters by Gustafson, Bill Moyers, Martha Mitchell, David Halliday, Greta Keller, Henry R. Luce, Sir Harold Acton, William Hurt, Harry Spiro, Kitty Carlisle Hart, Freddy Wittop, Calla Hay, Seymour Bernstein, Lloyd Moss, Maria Benitez, Miangul Aurangzeb, Valmai Howe Elkins, and Forrest Moses, among others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or folder title.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: Correspondence can also be found in Writings, Professional Papers, Personal Papers, and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Gorman and Gustafson in Italy, and friends such as Elizabeth Taylor and Ruth Warrick.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts, correspondence, annotated printouts, photographs, ephemera, and other materials related to books such as Cinderella is a Man, Last Guy Waltzing, India: Paradox and Treasures, and Expect the Unexpected, as well as travel articles by Gustafson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title.

did.unittitle: Assorted manuscripts: drafts and essays, including Robert Butts's lengthy descriptions of Apollo Muses
Ephemera, correspondence, book orders, and other materials
The Court Theaters of Europe , limited edition No. 1, signed
Expect the Unexpected , drafts, correspondence, and ephemera
Expect the Unexpected , printed version, signed
India: Paradox and Treasures , vol. I and II, monographs and ephemera
Last Guy Waltzing , notes, drafts, and photographs
Travel articles on Europe (Cornwall, Salzburg, London, Spain, Dalmatian Coast), and Costa Rica
Travel articles on India, Sri Lanka, and Nepal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of ephemera, clippings, photographs, correspondence, contracts, and other papers related to the Apollo Muses Center, exhibits curated by Gustafson, such as "Designs for a Prima Donna: Dame Joan Sutherland," and his lecture series on "The Court Theatres of Europe." Also included are research materials and a folio of photographs of India by Gustafson, papers related to a spiritual conference in Orissa, India, where he was a speaker, and a script for "The New Century," a play in which he starred as "Mr. Charles."

did.unittitle: Apollo Muses Center for the Arts
5th annual Apollo's Lyre, photographs, ephemera, and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a program, letters from Ruth Warrick, Celeste Holm, Jerome Hines, Toshiko Takaezu, and others, and pictures and publicity from the event, which honored Gustafson.

did.unittitle: Compass Gallery promotion and reviews; various photographs EG with Josette and Giorgetto in Tunisia and Italy [scrapbook]
"The Court Theaters of Europe" lecture materials
"Designs for a Prima Donna: Dame Joan Sutherland"
Early articles on Henry Luce and Claire Boothe Luce [research material]
"Ganesha and Friends: Images of India," limited edition folio, signed
India [photographs, clippings, notes, correspondence, and ephemera related to spiritual conference]
"The New Century," play by Paul Rudnick, ephemera and script
O. M. Miller manuscript (WWI, geographic explorations), O. M. Miller memorial program and concert, produced by EG
Santa Fe, New Mexico, Jamison Gallery, Pasa Por Aqui [clippings, photographs, and ephemera]
Theater design exhibition: various [clippings, ephemera, and photographs]
Various articles pertaining to EG: Doris Duke, theater design publicity, Art Unlimited , Vogue magazine, Art and Artists , Opera News , etc.
Walt Whitman Poetry Symposium "Vision of Democracy": press clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of materials related to Gustafson's early life, birthday celebrations, travels, and his stay at the Betty Ford Clinic. Included are a scrapbook, passports, calendars, address books, journals, and clippings on various subjects of personal interest to Gustafson.

did.unittitle: Assorted datebooks, notebooks, and address books
Assorted datebooks, notebooks, and address books
Betty Ford Clinic diary - daily comments by my councilor, Suzanne
EG's birthdays: 70th (London, Paris, Versailles), 75th (Corner House: 10-10-10 at 10), 80th (London, Paris, Versailles)
EG's early life: photographs, theater stubs, elementary, high school, and college ephemera [scrapbook]
Press clippings (obituaries: Elizabeth Taylor, Margaux Hemingway), "Nixon Quits"
Various artists [original artwork and greeting cards, photographs, ephemera, and clippings]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains photographs of friends, parties, performers, and concerts produced by the Apollo Muses Center; slides used in Gustafson's lectures "The Court Theatres of Europe"; and a scrapbook related to events in New York, the Santa Fe Opera, Gustafson's travels in the Southwest and California, and Indigenous American ceremonies. Some files include correspondence, ephemera, artwork, and writings.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: Photographs can also be found in Correspondence, Writings, Professional Papers, and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains performance and publicity stills, correspondence, clippings, and ephemera by or relating to Lillian Gish, Dame Joan Sutherland, Grace Bumbry, Rudolf Nureyev, Judith Jamison, Alvin Ailey, Risë Stevens, Catherine Deneuve, Ray Bradbury, and Beverly Sills, among others. Also included are photographs of Gustafson performing in various productions.

did.unittitle: Beginning concert of Apollo Muses at Lu Shan [with clippings and ephemera]
"The Court Theaters of Europe," slides and lecture notes
David Dynasty [photographs of David F. Wallace, David Scott Melville, and David McFadden]
EG's birthday party for Lila Tyng in Paris: large sheets with photographs, and pertinent ephemera
"Eric and Friends" at the Barge, December 1982: program and large sheets with photographs
Eric's men [with drawings, correspondence, and writings]
Large sheets of Xeroxed photographs [of Gustafson and friends in Spain, India, Sri Lanka, Cuba, and other locations]
"The Nightclub" at Lu Shan guest book/photographs
Photographs: Performers in Europe/Santa Fe, EG's Cooper-Hewitt Opening Night

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of "Operation Sail," an exhibition of tall ships in New York Harbor for the American Bicentennial; essay on the opening night of "Mother of Us All" at the Santa Fe Opera; and travel newsletters and photographs from Arizona, Santa Monica, California, and Picuris Pueblo and Santa Clara Pueblo in New Mexico; and poems written for Gustafson.

did.unittitle: Various photographs of friends, family, performers, and Lu Shan Farm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries primarily contains videocassette tapes of performances, workshops, and lectures at the Apollo Muses Center, and tapes of events or television shows featuring Toshiko Takaezu and R. C. Gorman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two subheadings: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, correspondence, photographs, ephemera, and other papers by or relating to Eric Gustafson. Included are research materials and drafts of My Beloved Southwest, Last Man Waltzing, and Cinderella is a Man; letters from friends, colleagues, and public figures such as R. C. Gorman, Frederica von Stade, Kitty Carlisle Hart, Dina Merrill, Robert F. Kennedy, Cecil Beaton, and Erté; a file on the death and estate of Lila Tyng; and catalogs and ephemera for festivals, exhibitions, and other events, such as the Festival of Two Worlds in Spoleto, Italy, which featured one of Gustafson's theater design exhibitions. The series also contains performance photographs or publicity stills of Alvin Ailey, Rosemary Clooney, Judith Jamison, and Frank Sinatra, among others, and photocopied photographs of Gustafson with Harry Truman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Eric Gustafson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

did.unittitle: Clippings, drafts, notes, ephemera, and photographs
Articles about REG, Calla Hay clippings REG, My Beloved Southwest materials
Travel articles - Santa Fe - Southwest - Taos - Canyon de Chelly et al.
Spoleto Festival program, photographs, and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a signed photograph of Willem de Kooning with Gian Carlo Menotti, and photograph of Muriel Costa-Greenspon performing as "The Medium" during the festival.

did.unittitle: Clarence Holbrook Carter catalogue, inscribed, with photograph
Poems, letters, drafts of "vignettes," and printed material
India travels, assorted articles, and correspondence
Website: Ericsjourney.com, and excerpts from Cinderella is a Man
Last Guy Waltzing , drafts, proofs, and email printouts
Misc - ephemera - "Happiness is a House…" letters, corrections, thoughts
Personal phone book from East 69th St/Gladstone days
Assorted letters, notes, photos and press clippings taken from autographed books
Letters from Cecil Beaton, Mary and Donald Oenslager, and University of North Dakota Department of Theatre Arts
Erté [Romain de Tirtoff] letters and ephemera
Ruth Page letters, ephemera, and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily letters from Ruth Page to Lila Tyng, with an early letter from Ruth's husband Tom Page to Lila and her first husband Harry Luce.

did.unittitle: Freddy Wittop letter and catalog for "Hello, Freddy: A retrospective of the career of Freddy Wittop."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains corrected typescript drafts and autograph manuscript notes for a book tentatively titled Diva, ultimately published as Last Man Waltzing.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Eric Gustafson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.
